description: 3.3V 1A low-dropout (LDO) linear voltage regulator — 1.2V dropout, SOT-223 or TO-252 package

pins.name: GND
pins.description: Ground / adjust pin. For fixed-voltage version, connect directly to GND.
pins.requirements: Connect to common GND. The exposed pad on SOT-223 (pin 2/tab) is also OUTPUT — do not confuse with GND.

pins.name: OUTPUT
pins.description: Regulated 3.3V output. In SOT-223 the large tab is also the OUTPUT — it can be soldered to a copper pour for heatsinking.
pins.requirements: 10µF tantalum or ceramic cap to GND required for stability. Place close to the pin.

pins.name: INPUT
pins.description: Unregulated input voltage. Must be at least 1.2V above output (4.5V minimum for 3.3V output).
pins.requirements: 10µF tantalum or ceramic cap to GND recommended for stability.

required_passives.reason: Required for stability — AMS1117 is unstable without output capacitor. Tantalum or low-ESR ceramic required.

application_circuits.name: Fixed 3.3V LDO
application_circuits.description: INPUT: 4.5V–15V with 10µF cap. OUTPUT: 3.3V with 10µF cap. GND pin to common ground.

common_mistakes: Output capacitor is required for stability — the AMS1117 will oscillate without it
SOT-223 large tab is OUTPUT not GND — check pinout carefully
Minimum input voltage is 4.5V for 3.3V output (1.2V dropout) — 5V input is the typical use case
Power dissipation at 5V input, 1A load = (5-3.3)×1 = 1.7W — exceeds SOT-223 rating without PCB copper heatsinking
AMS1117 versions differ: AMS1117-3.3 is fixed, AMS1117-ADJ is adjustable — part number suffix matters
Output capacitor must have adequate ESR — very low ESR ceramic caps can cause instability with some AMS1117 variants (check datasheet)
notes: Very common 3.3V regulator for 5V→3.3V conversion on Arduino, Raspberry Pi accessories, etc. The SOT-223 exposed pad provides some heatsinking when soldered to PCB copper. For heavy loads (>500mA) add copper pours or use TO-252 package. Equivalent: LD1117-3.3, LM1117-3.3.
